answer:
To estimate the walking speed of T. rex, we model its leg as a physical pendulum with length \( L = 3.1 \, \text{m} \). The natural walking frequency \( f \) of a pendulum is approximately \( f = \frac{1}{2\pi} \sqrt{\frac{g}{L}} \), where \( g = 9.8 \, \text{m/s}^2 \). Given a stride length \( S = 4.0 \, \text{m} \), the speed \( v = f \times S \approx 1.4 \, \text{m/s} \).

\boxed{C}
